items.headline: Geopolitical Tensions and Weak Jobs Data Fuel 'Risk-Off' Sentiment
items.summary: U.S. equities concluded the week ending March 7, 2026, lower across all major indices as escalating geopolitical tensions in the Middle East drove a sharp rise in crude oil prices, coupled with a weaker-than-expected U.S. employment report. This combination fostered a 'risk-off' environment, particularly impacting small and mid-cap stocks. Investors are also digesting ongoing inflation concerns and the Federal Reserve's stance on interest rates, with expectations firm for no change at the upcoming FOMC meeting. Not financial advice.

items.headline: Gemini Space Station Soars
items.summary: Smallcap stock Gemini Space Station led gainers with a significant jump.

items.headline: Circle Internet Group Rises Amid Crypto Volatility
items.summary: Blockchain-native capital marketplace Circle Internet Group saw strong gains.

items.headline: Microsoft Posts Weekly Gain Despite Regulatory Scrutiny
items.summary: Microsoft shares advanced, rebounding amidst broader software stock struggles.

items.headline: Lumentum Holdings Plunges
items.summary: Lumentum Holdings was among the week's worst performers.

items.headline: Rocket Companies Dips on Partnership News
items.summary: Rocket Companies shed value after a competitor's AI partnership announcement.

items.headline: MongoDB Falls on Weak Guidance
items.summary: MongoDB shares slumped following disappointing Q1 revenue guidance.

items.headline: Sherwin-Williams Declines as Consumer Staples Face Pressure
items.summary: Consumer staples stock Sherwin-Williams saw a notable decline.

items.headline: U.S. Economy Loses 92,000 Jobs in February
items.summary: The U.S. labor market contracted sharply in February, with the economy losing 92,000 nonfarm payrolls and the unemployment rate climbing to 4.4%. This data, contrary to expectations of job growth, intensified concerns about an economic slowdown.

items.headline: Middle East Conflict Drives Oil Prices Above $90, Market Uncertainty Soars
items.summary: Crude oil prices surged by 35.5%, finishing the week near $91 per barrel, as an escalating conflict in the Middle East, triggered by the killing of an Iranian Supreme Leader, raised fears over oil supply chains. This geopolitical turmoil has created significant market uncertainty and contributed to inflationary pressures.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Expected to Hold Rates Steady in March
items.summary: Despite recent economic data, market consensus strongly indicates the Federal Reserve will maintain its benchmark interest rate at 3.50%-3.75% at the upcoming FOMC meeting on March 17-18, 2026. While one or two rate cuts are anticipated for 2026, they are generally not expected until summer at the earliest.

items.headline: Software Stocks See Rebound Amid Sector Rotation
items.summary: Amid the broader market downturn, software-adjacent stocks like IBM, Microsoft, and Salesforce experienced a rebound last week. This indicates a rotational trade as investors moved away from consumer staples and manufacturing names, which faced pressure from rising costs and economic uncertainty.
